programme.title: W5
programme.desc: W5 investigates a three-decades-old cold case - the unsolved murder of a young boy.

programme.sub-title: CTV National News for Thursday, May 8, 2025

programme.title: Fish The Dish
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts creates some dairy-infused dishes using red snapper and scampi.

programme.title: Fish The Dish
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts creates recipes that combine sour and tart flavors with grouper clams.

programme.title: Fish The Dish
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts demonstrates how a little caffeinated flavour can bring your fish dish to the next level.

programme.title: Fish The Dish
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts shows how you can blend meat and seafood flavours to create tantalizing surf and turf dishes.

programme.title: Fun'Q with Spencer Watts
programme.desc: Bring on the face wiping, finger licking goodness with messy and delicious, fall off the bone, soya beef ribs with sesame mayo.

programme.title: Fun'Q with Spencer Watts
programme.desc: Spencer Watts is inspired to embrace big, big food for a big big barbecue feast.

programme.title: Fun'Q with Spencer Watts
programme.desc: Delicious grilled food is good for morning, noon and night, so let’s Brunch.

programme.title: Fun'Q with Spencer Watts
programme.desc: Spencer’s feeling spicy! He makes a light chickpea salad with charred halloumi and a cumin vinaigrette.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts makes a full, family-style Indian meal in around 30 minutes.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: Why order take-out when you can make it fresher, faster, and even healthier at home?

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: It's Tandoori Take-Out night as host Spencer Watts prepares five quick and easy Indian dishes that take little time, but are big in flavour.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts prepares a delicious Chinese Take-Out meal at home in just 30 minutes.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: Thai food is on the menu as Chef Spencer Watts cooks up an affordable dinner in around 30 minutes.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts makes fast and affordable Japanese food for four, all in around 30 minutes.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts makes a fresh and fragrant Vietnamese meal for four for around 30 dollars.

programme.title: Fish The Dish
programme.desc: Spencer spices things up as he makes pepper-inspired dishes with catfish and clams.

programme.title: Fish The Dish
programme.desc: Spencer dresses sablefish in a mole sauce that has a hint of maple and chocolate; lobster has its sweet side.

programme.title: Fish The Dish
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts cooks up fast faves using scallops and trout, all done in under 30 minutes.

programme.title: Fish The Dish
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts mixes the sweet flavors of fruit with fish as he makes dishes with salmon and scallops.

programme.title: Fish The Dish
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts creates some dairy-infused dishes using red snapper and scampi.

programme.title: Fish The Dish
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts creates recipes that combine sour and tart flavors with grouper clams.

programme.title: Fish The Dish
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts demonstrates how a little caffeinated flavour can bring your fish dish to the next level.

programme.title: Fish The Dish
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts shows how you can blend meat and seafood flavours to create tantalizing surf and turf dishes.

programme.title: Fun'Q with Spencer Watts
programme.desc: Bring on the face wiping, finger licking goodness with messy and delicious, fall off the bone, soya beef ribs with sesame mayo.

programme.title: Fun'Q with Spencer Watts
programme.desc: Spencer Watts is inspired to embrace big, big food for a big big barbecue feast.

programme.title: Fun'Q with Spencer Watts
programme.desc: Delicious grilled food is good for morning, noon and night, so let’s Brunch.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Vietnamese Food With a French Twist
programme.desc: Vietnamese dishes with French influence.

programme.title: Up The Dish
programme.desc: Up the Dish's four chefs take a shot at dumplings, from classic Americana to upscale European!

programme.title: Up The Dish
programme.desc: Up the Dish's four chefs go raw – and raw vegan – with their variations on a classic Japanese dish.

programme.title: Up The Dish
programme.desc: Up the Dish's four chefs offer worldwide inspirations on a classic rice dish.

programme.title: Up The Dish
programme.desc: Up the Dish's four chefs begin in East Asia with classic Pad Thai then make twists inspired by the classic.

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: Bianca Osbourne combines the cuisines of Italy and China to create inventive dishes.

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: Host Bianca Osbourne combines the cuisines of Georgia and Hong Kong to create delicious dishes.

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: Host Bianca Osbourne combines the cuisines of South Korea and Spain to create inventive dishes.

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: Host Bianca Osbourne celebrates the tastes of Morocco and Thailand and creates delicious dishes combining their cuisines.

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: Chef Craig Wong combines the cuisines of Vietnam and France to create delicious dishes.

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: Chef Craig Wong celebrates the tastes of India and the USA and creates delicious dishes combining their cuisines.

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: Chef Craig Wong combines the tastes of Ireland and Japan to create delicious pub food fare.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Hana Etsuko Dethlefsen fixes Japanese comfort food.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Thai chef Pailin Chongchitnant shows us the secrets behind Bangkok’s famous street food.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Japanese Recipes Inspired by Western Cuisine
programme.desc: Chef and cookbook author Hana Etsuko Dethlefsen makes recipes that are inspired by western cuisine

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef and cookbook author Hana Etsuko Dethlefsen introduces us to some of the most popular noodles in Japanese cuisine.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Thai chef and cookbook author Pailin Chongchitnant takes us on a culinary trip of Chiang Mai.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Hana teaches us the secrets behind forming the delicate tofu pockets, and why the pockets are named after the Shinto god Inari.

programme.title: Fun'Q with Spencer Watts
programme.desc: The sun is shining and Spencer is turning up the heat, cooking delicious grilled food perfect to be served on a bun.

programme.title: Fun'Q with Spencer Watts
programme.desc: Spencer is inspired by all the fun different flavours you can bring to food with peppers.

programme.title: Fun'Q with Spencer Watts
programme.desc: Big platters of food, lots of smiling faces, sharing and laughter - who doesn't love a great grilled feast?

programme.title: Fun'Q with Spencer Watts
programme.desc: Fire and meat has been done since the caveman era - but vegetarian dishes - that's where things can get really creative.

programme.title: Fun'Q with Spencer Watts
programme.desc: Spencer celebrates his favourite vacation spot with a Hawaiian themed barbecued feast.

programme.title: Fun'Q with Spencer Watts
programme.desc: Indian and Asian spices are versatile and delicious, especially when you add a little char from the barbecue.

programme.title: Fun'Q with Spencer Watts
programme.desc: The best grilled treats sometime come in bite sizes portions so Spencer is making grilled appetizers!

programme.title: Fun'Q with Spencer Watts
programme.desc: Spencer embraces the bounty of herbs available in the summer and makes lavender salted spatchcock chicken with a balsamic truffle glaze.

programme.title: Fun'Q with Spencer Watts
programme.desc: A fiesta is a celebration and Spencer celebrates with some delicious grilled food!

programme.title: Comfort Food With Spencer Watts
programme.desc: Making black bean and kabocha squash soup with braised Swiss chard and chili pesto.

programme.title: Comfort Food With Spencer Watts
programme.desc: Spencer makes a spicy spinach and Taleggio cheese wonton soup in a chorizo broth.

programme.title: Comfort Food With Spencer Watts
programme.desc: A pork bolognese stuffed tortellini with American cheese rose and soffrito.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: Earth's new generation of orbiting telescopes promises to expand our universe of knowledge about our origins.

programme.title: Hurricane: The Anatomy
programme.desc: This episode explores the search for hurricane scientists' holy grail.

programme.title: Hurricane: The Anatomy
programme.desc: This episode looks at how plants and animals cope with the annual onslaught in the hurricane zone.

programme.title: Hurricane: The Anatomy
programme.desc: With coastal habitats and natural defenses irretrievably damaged, the future appears bleak.

programme.title: Life in Outer Space
programme.desc: Scientists are exploring planets and moons that may once have supported life. Saturn's moon, Europa, is one.

programme.title: Planet Rethink
programme.desc: Pollution is everywhere. But all is not dire. We need to increase resource efficiency to preserve our planet.

programme.title: Shockwave
programme.desc: A powerful earthquake and tsunami will some day devastate the Pacific Northwest. What can we do to prepare?

programme.title: Solar Superstorms: Journey to the Center of the Sun
programme.desc: As technology and electrical grids have spread, big solar storms pose a threat equal to the worst hurricanes.

programme.title: Day of the Asteroid
programme.desc: Armed with new defensive technologies, scientists are getting ready for the Day of the Asteroid.

programme.title: Exoplanets: Thousands of New Worlds
programme.desc: Are we alone? Scientists challenge traditional planetary science to seek and observe worlds called exoplanets.

programme.title: Earth's Survival: Decoding the Science
programme.desc: This documentary explains how we may be in the middle of the most crucial moment of Earth’s history.

programme.title: Hubble: Eye in the Sky
programme.desc: In this beautiful show, viewers get an inside look at the challenges of operating the Hubble telescope.

programme.title: Expedition Antarctica
programme.desc: A hardy research crew seeks clues about the fate of our planet beneath the icy waters of the Antarctic Ocean.

programme.title: The Jupiter Enigma
programme.desc: NASA’s Juno mission to Jupiter is revealing the origins of our solar system, when Earth's fate was uncertain.

programme.title: The Saturn V Story
programme.desc: This is the story of the most powerful machine ever built and a nation who watched on with hope, together.

programme.title: Mars Calling: Manifest Destiny or Grand Illusion
programme.desc: This film merges the mythic and the scientific to show that Mars may not be what we imagine.

programme.title: The Next Great Extinction Event
programme.desc: There have been five major extinction events in our planet's history, are we about to experience the 6th?

programme.title: Faster Than Light: the Dream of Interstellar Flight
programme.desc: Next-generation rocket technology is fueling the dream of traveling across the void.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: We're looking hard, but the numbers aren't stacking up. Is life on other planets really that hard to find?

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: Two new space probes can now orbit closer to the Sun than ever before, enabling groundbreaking research.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: Scientists are studying the benefits of a hybrid moon mission, one where humans and robots work in tandem.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: Climate change is causing increasing havoc to our environment. How can science respond to this challenge?

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: Venus may be considered Earth's evil twin. Hostile and unforgiving, Venus is the subject of intense scrutiny.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: The International Space Station is now the only manned station in space, but soon there may be more.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: Asteroids are everywhere in space. We count them, examine why they exist, and track them for Earth's safety.

programme.title: Voyage of Darwin's Beagle: On The Future of Species
programme.desc: About 180 years after Darwin, his great-granddaughter follows his path, traveling from Tenerife to the Amazon.

programme.title: Voyage of Darwin's Beagle: On The Future of Species
programme.sub-title: Low Life on the Ladder of God
programme.desc: Follow Darwin's journey to see remnants of slavery and to relive his discovery of fossils of extinct animals.

programme.title: Planet Rethink
programme.desc: Pollution is everywhere. But all is not dire. We need to increase resource efficiency to preserve our planet.

programme.title: Antarctica
programme.desc: Explore Antarctica, one of the world's most beautiful, yet inhospitable, landscapes.

programme.title: Facing Forces of Nature
programme.desc: An asteroid could be barreling toward Earth at any time, now or in the future. Only science can save us.

programme.title: Facing Forces of Nature
programme.desc: Tornadoes: Can we use modern technology to predict the emergence and path of this violent force of nature?

programme.title: Facing Forces of Nature
programme.desc: Volcanoes: How successful can we be in the fight against this violent, destructive, elemental force of nature?

programme.title: Facing Forces of Nature
programme.desc: Melting ice at Earth's poles: Do we still have a chance to stop it from melting and prevent its repercussions?

programme.title: Wild Faces of Switzerland
programme.desc: Take an exciting journey of discovery into an almost untouched wilderness: The Jura Mountains.

programme.title: Wild Faces of Switzerland
programme.desc: This episode focuses on Ticino, reaching from the Tessin Alps down to the charming Muggio Valley.

programme.title: Wild Faces of Switzerland
programme.desc: Take an amazing journey from the highest peaks of the Bernese Alps to a spectacular finish at the Rhine Falls.

programme.title: Wild Faces of Switzerland
programme.desc: The ibex, marmot, and European adder have all developed strategies to survive the harsh alpine winter.

programme.title: Mega Disaster
programme.desc: Volcanoes can bury entire cities in clouds of ash and gas, or turn rivers into torrents of mud and debris.

programme.title: Mega Disaster
programme.desc: There is no stopping these massive and deadly phenomena, the only thing to do is run to higher ground.

programme.title: Mega Disaster
programme.desc: From the tremendous, to the terrifying, to the tragic, this is a countdown to a mega tornado.

programme.title: Mega Disaster
programme.desc: Earthquakes baffle experts as they try to gather the information necessary to predict the next big one.

programme.title: Earth in 1000 years
programme.desc: Earth's stores of ice on Greenland and Antarctica are melting, and sea levels rising. Where is Earth headed?

programme.title: Antarctica
programme.desc: Explore Antarctica, one of the world's most beautiful, yet inhospitable, landscapes.

programme.title: Facing Forces of Nature
programme.desc: An asteroid could be barreling toward Earth at any time, now or in the future. Only science can save us.

programme.title: Facing Forces of Nature
programme.desc: Tornadoes: Can we use modern technology to predict the emergence and path of this violent force of nature?

programme.title: Facing Forces of Nature
programme.desc: Volcanoes: How successful can we be in the fight against this violent, destructive, elemental force of nature?

programme.title: Facing Forces of Nature
programme.desc: Melting ice at Earth's poles: Do we still have a chance to stop it from melting and prevent its repercussions?

programme.title: Wild Faces of Switzerland
programme.desc: Take an exciting journey of discovery into an almost untouched wilderness: The Jura Mountains.

programme.title: Wild Faces of Switzerland
programme.desc: This episode focuses on Ticino, reaching from the Tessin Alps down to the charming Muggio Valley.

programme.title: Wild Faces of Switzerland
programme.desc: Take an amazing journey from the highest peaks of the Bernese Alps to a spectacular finish at the Rhine Falls.

programme.title: Wild Faces of Switzerland
programme.desc: The ibex, marmot, and European adder have all developed strategies to survive the harsh alpine winter.

programme.title: Fascinating Places
programme.desc: The West Coast of Madagascar is remote and inhospitable but home to an astonishing array of animals.

programme.title: Fascinating Places
programme.desc: The Northeast of Madagascar is a major trading center and one of the most species-rich regions in the world.

programme.title: Fascinating Places
programme.desc: Madagascar is an island paradise in the Indian Ocean, home to fun-loving folk and a unique natural landscape.

programme.title: Fascinating Places
programme.desc: The Black Forest captivates all who visit and has a reputation for its unique landscape and inhabitants.

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: From the earth to the stars to the edge of the unknown, explore the mystery, majesty and power of the world we live in and beyond.

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: From the earth to the stars to the edge of the unknown, explore the mystery, majesty and power of the world we live in and beyond.

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: From the earth to the stars to the edge of the unknown, explore the mystery, majesty and power of the world we live in and beyond.

programme.title: Evolution Earth

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: From the earth to the stars to the edge of the unknown, explore the mystery, majesty and power of the world we live in and beyond.

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: From the earth to the stars to the edge of the unknown, explore the mystery, majesty and power of the world we live in and beyond.

programme.title: Over the Islands of Africa
programme.sub-title: Over the Islands of Africa, Madagascar
programme.desc: Spirits, Kings, Lemurs – Madagascar treats Ami to a multitude of new impressions and spectacular pictures. Director Christian Schidlowski and his team accompanied her on her trip.

programme.title: Over the Islands of Africa
programme.sub-title: Over the Islands of Africa, Sao Tome and Principe
programme.desc: Along with photographer Rui Camilo, we travel to the West African island nation of Sao Tomé and Príncipe - in search of images that will capture the soul of this small and beautiful country.

programme.title: Over the Islands of Africa
programme.sub-title: Over the Islands of Africa, Cape Verde
programme.desc: Cape Verde – the islands of Saudade, of longing. For Stéphane and Richard it was a journey full of adventure, unforgettable encounters and magnificent photographs. Director Christian Schidlowski and his team accompanied them on their trip.

programme.title: Bosnia Herzegovina
programme.desc: Wild and unknown lies the still undiscovered natural paradise of Bosnia and Herzegovina in the heart of Europe. Rough mountains, dense forests and wild waterfalls characterize the picture of the untouched landscapes. Lynx, wolves and bears roam mountains and valleys. One of the last primeval forests in Europe is a retreat for many endangered animal species. Bosnia and Herzegovina is actually an undiscovered natural paradise in the middle of Europe.

programme.title: Bosnia Herzegovina
programme.desc: The barren, karstic south of the country, Herzegovina, also impresses with its natural landscapes. One of them is the Livno field. Not only is it the largest wetland in the country, it is also the largest regularly flooded karst field in the world. About 700 wild horses live in this unique landscape. Their ancestors were working horses that were abandoned here more than 50 years ago. Another special feature of the karst landscape are the rivers with their tufa barriers. The countless waterfalls that pour over it are a unique natural phenomenon.

programme.title: Wilderness in Europe: The Bohemian Forest
programme.desc: Pavel Hubeny, whose life would be unthinkable without the forest, is the head of the Šumava National Park. He is committed to ensuring that a primeval forest can grow for future generations, in which man does not interfere. This vision of a primeval forest is also shared by the forester Claus Bässler, who wants to counteract the extinction of species by having trees felled to create artificial deadwood and thus a new habitat for rare beetles and fungi. On the Vltava River lies the small town of Krumlov, the "Pearl of the Bohemian Forest" with its Baroque theater and fully preserved stage technology from the 17th century. Here the director Zuzana Vrbová enchants people from all over the world with her productions. Apart from agriculture, it was mainly the abundance of forests that ensured the livelihood of the inhabitants of the Bohemian Forest - including that of the carpenter Philipp von Manz, who processed local wood for his customers all over Germany. Veterinarian Judith Gollner, who grew up in the Austrian part of the Bohemian Forest, lived in Vienna for a long time and made a conscious decision to return. She likes the people and is proud of "her farmers' wives" who share her love of the Bohemian Forest.

programme.title: Vicious Beauties - The secret World of the Jelly Fish
programme.desc: Watching the graceful movement of these gelatinous animals is almost hypnotic. Although their bodies are 98% water, jellyfish are deceptively clever and dangerous. But where do these primordial creatures come from? How do they reproduce and how long do they live?

programme.title: Predators of a Different Kind - In the World of the Unknown Sharks
programme.desc: With the aid of modern technology, reveal the unknown behaviour of some unusual species of shark: lemon sharks and their white-tip reef counterparts. Our film trip begins on the Bahamas. To be more precise: in the turquoise-blue waters of the Grand Bahama Bank, in an exactly fixed location. In the spring, 70 – 80 pregnant lemon sharks arrive here. This huge shark population was first discovered just a few years ago by shark researcher, Professor Sam Gruber. Many of the females are tired and rest on the seabed. Pregnant tiger sharks swim in the midst of this group. Without any protection whatsoever, our cameramen shoot their footage, surrounded by sharks and succeed in capturing images hitherto unseen. We continue our journey to Gainesville, Florida, to meet Gordon Hubbell, the leading shark denture expert with the world’s largest shark denture collection. He knows everything about the evolutionary history of the lemon sharks, in addition to those of the white-tip reef variety off Cocos Island, Costa Rica. Here, we encounter large schools of fish, unprecedented numbers of stingrays, as well as hammerhead sharks. However, it is the white-tip reek sharks that make the biggest impression. Their performance begins late at night. Marauding, they patrol in large groups through the reefs and hunt everything that moves. Scales and dead prey fish float above the reef – a welcoming change of diet for the ubiquitous barracudas.

programme.title: Giants of Fortune - The Whale Sharks of Oslob
programme.desc: In the little Filipino village of Oslob on the Cebu Island, people suffer from typhoons and overfishing. Until the world’s biggest fish and the Internet change everything.Fisherman Zosimo’s dearest wish is for his children to graduate from high school one day. Yet the chance that this wish will come true is tiny, since at times Zosimo doesn’t even manage to feed the family. Typhoons and overfishing make the lives of Oslob fishermen difficult.

programme.title: The Oceans Seven - The Marathon Swimming Challenge
programme.desc: André Wiersig, born in 1972, has achieved what no German has ever done before: he has swum through the world's seven most famous straits, the so-called Ocean's Seven. To prepare for it, he chose a familiar but hostile environment. In his carport, he hardened himself in a rain barrel filled with groundwater.

programme.title: Symphony of the Seas - Underwater Wonders of Australia's Ocean
programme.desc: Our journeys take us all around the world from Patagonia to the USA, from Greenland to South Africa and from Cambodia to Australia and leads us to the most fascinating places where landscapes animals and humans are the main focus of our documentaries. Whether on land or at sea – the last paradises of the world are our destination.

programme.title: The Last Paradises: Patagonia - Coast of the Killer Whale
programme.desc: On the wild coast of Patagonia, the nature calendar describes the most diverse visiting times of fascinating animals, from the southern right whale and other large whales, via the sea lions and elephant seals up to the orcas and millions of penguins. The encounters with whales and their babies are spectacular. Hardly anywhere else on earth can one experience both the brutal and the delightful sides of nature so vividly and so close. The trip to the hot spots for animal watchers and researchers leads us inter alia to the Caleta Valdez. This is where, during the mating season, the southern elephant seals bring their babies into the world, mate and enjoy a little sunbathing on the beach.

programme.title: Wild Pacific - The Beauty of Life
programme.desc: The Pacific Ocean is the largest and deepest of Earth's oceanic divisions. It extends from the Arctic Ocean in the north to the Southern Ocean.

programme.title: Secrets of the North Sea
programme.desc: The North Sea is enigmatic. It presents itself in cool shades of grey. Yet who would have guessed that beneath the chilly waves are opulent coral gardens, courting squids, and several of the largest sea creatures on the planet?

programme.title: Mysterious Oceanic Oases - Hotspots of Life
programme.desc: Countless marine animals frolic on the steep slopes of underwater volcanoes. In many cases, several are rare species not found elsewhere. This is in part due to the fact that plankton clings to the wall and provides the fish with food in abundance. This documentary shows several of these oases and also examines the impact dive tourism has and whether preventive measures can help to maintain them. Nature and underwater filmmaker Rolf Möltgen undertakes dives into the mysterious underwater mountain landscapes with marine scientists from all corners of the globe. This journey takes us to Mexico, to snappers, white-tip reef sharks, Galapagos sharks and giant mantas. We visit the Maldives, Colombia and Costa Rica, where the team documents rare species such as deep sea burbots, small tooth sand tiger and dogfish sharks, in depths of more than 3 metres. The eerie underwater mountain world proves: everyone plays a part in the network of life. Thanks to consistent protective regulations, it has been possible to retain the underwater diversity - especially in the unique oases of the high seas

programme.title: Secrets of the Mediterranean
programme.desc: The contours of the Mediterranean have changed during the course of its history. Near Gibraltar, Africa was once connected to the European continent. When the land bridge at Gibraltar collapsed, the Mediterranean basin filled up again. The Suez Canal is today connected to the Red Sea. This route enables animals to reach the Mediterranean, where they cause problems.

programme.title: Secrets of the Mediterranean
programme.desc: Rugged rocks, blue sea and idyllic, sandy beaches - this is the Mediterranean as we know it. But this impression is deceptive: the Mediterranean is under constant change. The continents are moving towards one another. Animals, that have to survive under such extreme conditions, must find a way to escape the dangers. Between deserts and volcanoes, the challenges for the animals are enormous.

programme.title: Europe's far North - Lofoten and the Arctic Ocean
programme.desc: Lofoten is the rugged archipelago in the Norwegian Arctic Ocean. Whoever arrives here is really at the end. Moskenes is the end of the line, and anyone who wants to go further needs a boat or a plane. The only way to get to the open sea is via the Maelstrom, which is quite dangerous. On the outermost headland, there are only a few ruins left. The last inhabitants are long gone, a supply was hardly possible. But fishermen are on the way, mainly to catch cod. And there is the coastal administration, which takes care of broken sea marks and lighthouses. A visit to one of the luxurious ships of the Hurtig Line is not to be missed. They shuttle off rough coasts on an eleven-day trip between the Russian border and southern Norway. A floating workplace for a wide variety of professions. The island of Andøya is not served by the Hurtiglinie. It lies too far out in the Atlantic. One of the most important observatories in Europe is located here. A young woman, Sandra Blindheim, is the boss. She is responsible for the large laser that delivers important information to scientists around the world. On clear winter nights, there is a wonderful panoramic view of the aurora borealis.

programme.title: Jacques Cousteau's Legacy - Return To The Undersea World
programme.desc: A composition of wildlife footage, archive material and elaborate re-enactments results in a brilliant reconstruction of the life of famed marine biologist and environmental pioneer Jacques Cousteau. On the trail of the most famous oceanographer of the 20th Century, the team spent two years filming original locations on three different continents, in four different oceans. The film charts how Cousteau’s outlook on the environment changed throughout his many decades at sea around the globe. On his personal journey to enlightenment, he changed from a man who killed sharks in hate and thoughtlessly blew up coral reefs, to a radical environmentalist.

programme.title: Realm of the robber - Christmas Island
programme.desc: Thievery and treachery are second nature for the world's largest land crab. This documentary explores the untold story of Christmas Island's iconic robber crabs. Armed with claws that deliver more force than a lion's bite, they can grow a metre across and are relentless in their search for food. However, the brash crustaceans have surprisingly complex private lives and often find themselves in precarious situations, whether escaping from frenzied ants or risking their life to deposit fertilised eggs into a raging ocean. To tell the story of the robber crab's life cycle, directors Moritz Katz and Braydon Moloney spent a year in the field, following their subjects in minute detail. The tragedies, trials and triumphs paint a fresh, humorous portrait of Christmas Island and its nefarious denizens.

programme.title: Lobsters - The Knights Of The Deep
programme.desc: The knights of the deep are on the move; almost no part of their bodies is without armor. Like medieval warriors, these fighters are well armed – not with sword and shield, but with scissors and forceps and defensive armor that is covered with spikes and hooks. And even though they are well protected and have an uncanny awareness of their surroundings, many of these proud knights will face a gloomy destiny. Many of them will end upon a plate. Gourmets all over the world are in love with these famous crustaceans: lobsters! For marine biologists lobsters are similarly fascinating: as research objects. We are on their trail in Cape Breton, an island in the northern Atlantic and part of the Canadian province Nova Scotia, where one of the largest populations of lobsters has its home.

programme.title: Vicious Beauties - The secret World of the Jelly Fish
programme.desc: Watching the graceful movement of these gelatinous animals is almost hypnotic. Although their bodies are 98% water, jellyfish are deceptively clever and dangerous. But where do these primordial creatures come from? How do they reproduce and how long do they live?

programme.title: Predators of a Different Kind - In the World of the Unknown Sharks
programme.desc: With the aid of modern technology, reveal the unknown behaviour of some unusual species of shark: lemon sharks and their white-tip reef counterparts. Our film trip begins on the Bahamas. To be more precise: in the turquoise-blue waters of the Grand Bahama Bank, in an exactly fixed location. In the spring, 70 – 80 pregnant lemon sharks arrive here. This huge shark population was first discovered just a few years ago by shark researcher, Professor Sam Gruber. Many of the females are tired and rest on the seabed. Pregnant tiger sharks swim in the midst of this group. Without any protection whatsoever, our cameramen shoot their footage, surrounded by sharks and succeed in capturing images hitherto unseen. We continue our journey to Gainesville, Florida, to meet Gordon Hubbell, the leading shark denture expert with the world’s largest shark denture collection. He knows everything about the evolutionary history of the lemon sharks, in addition to those of the white-tip reef variety off Cocos Island, Costa Rica. Here, we encounter large schools of fish, unprecedented numbers of stingrays, as well as hammerhead sharks. However, it is the white-tip reek sharks that make the biggest impression. Their performance begins late at night. Marauding, they patrol in large groups through the reefs and hunt everything that moves. Scales and dead prey fish float above the reef – a welcoming change of diet for the ubiquitous barracudas.

programme.title: Giants of Fortune - The Whale Sharks of Oslob
programme.desc: In the little Filipino village of Oslob on the Cebu Island, people suffer from typhoons and overfishing. Until the world’s biggest fish and the Internet change everything.Fisherman Zosimo’s dearest wish is for his children to graduate from high school one day. Yet the chance that this wish will come true is tiny, since at times Zosimo doesn’t even manage to feed the family. Typhoons and overfishing make the lives of Oslob fishermen difficult.

programme.title: The Oceans Seven - The Marathon Swimming Challenge
programme.desc: André Wiersig, born in 1972, has achieved what no German has ever done before: he has swum through the world's seven most famous straits, the so-called Ocean's Seven. To prepare for it, he chose a familiar but hostile environment. In his carport, he hardened himself in a rain barrel filled with groundwater.

programme.title: Symphony of the Seas - Underwater Wonders of Australia's Ocean
programme.desc: Our journeys take us all around the world from Patagonia to the USA, from Greenland to South Africa and from Cambodia to Australia and leads us to the most fascinating places where landscapes animals and humans are the main focus of our documentaries. Whether on land or at sea – the last paradises of the world are our destination.

programme.title: The Last Paradises: Patagonia - Coast of the Killer Whale
programme.desc: On the wild coast of Patagonia, the nature calendar describes the most diverse visiting times of fascinating animals, from the southern right whale and other large whales, via the sea lions and elephant seals up to the orcas and millions of penguins. The encounters with whales and their babies are spectacular. Hardly anywhere else on earth can one experience both the brutal and the delightful sides of nature so vividly and so close. The trip to the hot spots for animal watchers and researchers leads us inter alia to the Caleta Valdez. This is where, during the mating season, the southern elephant seals bring their babies into the world, mate and enjoy a little sunbathing on the beach.

programme.title: Parrot's Paradise
programme.desc: Aaron Pedersen explores the unique Australian characteristics that have facilitated the thriving of parrots

programme.title: Super Senses
programme.desc: Living in the aerial realm requires a unique set of senses

programme.title: Eagle - Queen Of The Skies
programme.desc: To explore the world of the eagle we follow the fortunes of one individual from hatching to leaving the nest.

programme.title: Storks - A Village Rooftop Saga
programme.desc: Ruhstadt, Germany, hosts Germany's largest white stork colony, despite facing damage to roads, roofs

programme.title: Circus In The Sky
programme.desc: With their acrobatic aerial plays, birds of prey make some of the most impressive appearances in the sky.

programme.title: Yellowstone Journals
programme.desc: Yellowstone's creatures thrive due to unique abilities, anatomies, and senses, especially in Spring.

programme.title: The Birds of Winter
programme.desc: Birds, including Arctic foxes, cranes, and swans, prepare for their winter journey.

programme.title: To the Ends of the Earth
programme.desc: Discover East Africa’s incredible landscapes and the amazing birds that inhabit it.

programme.title: Biebrza - The European Amazon
programme.desc: A unique story about Biebrza - the European Amazon

programme.title: Penguin Baywatch
programme.desc: King penguins, seals, and other sea animals face breeding challenges, including inbreeding.

programme.title: Penguin Baywatch
programme.desc: Macaroni penguins in Antarctica form strong family bonds, facing predators and playful fur seals.

programme.title: Super Senses
programme.desc: In this episode we'll explore the animal sense of sight

programme.title: Beak & Brain - Genius Birds From Down Under
programme.desc: These child prodigies of the animal kingdom have feathers and beaks - and very special skills!

programme.title: Parrot's Paradise
programme.desc: Aaron Pedersen explores the unique Australian characteristics that have facilitated the thriving of parrots

programme.title: Super Senses
programme.desc: Living in the aerial realm requires a unique set of senses

programme.title: Eagle - Queen Of The Skies
programme.desc: To explore the world of the eagle we follow the fortunes of one individual from hatching to leaving the nest.

programme.title: Storks - A Village Rooftop Saga
programme.desc: Ruhstadt, Germany, hosts Germany's largest white stork colony, despite facing damage to roads, roofs

programme.title: Circus In The Sky
programme.desc: With their acrobatic aerial plays, birds of prey make some of the most impressive appearances in the sky.

programme.title: Yellowstone Journals
programme.desc: Yellowstone's creatures thrive due to unique abilities, anatomies, and senses, especially in Spring.

programme.title: The Birds of Winter
programme.desc: Birds, including Arctic foxes, cranes, and swans, prepare for their winter journey.

programme.title: Super Senses
programme.desc: We travel from the desert to the forest understanding how creatures never miss an auditory trick

programme.title: Beasts & Witches
programme.desc: The forest is the place of great legends.

programme.title: Animal Special Forces
programme.desc: These animals excel in patrol and pursuit, using various tactics like trap setting

programme.title: Keas - New Zealand'S Witty Daredevils
programme.desc: The Keas, New Zealand's intelligent mountain parrots, are known for their love for snow

programme.title: Aerial Assassins
programme.desc: James Currie is embarking on a mission to witness the majestic Harris Hawk

programme.title: Beasts & Witches
programme.desc: Time to discover these farm animal’s extraordinary hidden lives. No evil spell to fear here!

programme.title: Hunters Of The Skies
programme.desc: Hawks, buzzards, and eagles are powerful birds with diverse hunting strategies, conquering various habitats

programme.title: The Tick and the Bird
programme.desc: A flea in a Laysan Albatross' feathers crosses the Pacific, landing on Midway Island to mate & form a family.

programme.title: The Breakdown
programme.desc: Make sense of your world with original reporting that breaks down the world's biggest news stories.

programme.title: CBC News Here and Now
programme.desc: Carolyn Stokes and Ashley Brauweiler deliver a complete package of local news and weather.

programme.title: CBC News Here and Now
programme.desc: Carolyn Stokes and Ashley Brauweiler deliver a complete package of local news and weather.

programme.title: CBC News Here and Now
programme.desc: Carolyn Stokes and Ashley Brauweiler deliver a complete package of local news and weather.

programme.title: CBC News Here and Now
programme.desc: Carolyn Stokes and Ashley Brauweiler deliver a complete package of local news and weather.

programme.title: About That
programme.desc: Andrew Chang expands our understanding of the stories everybody's talking about.

programme.title: The National
programme.desc: The National is CBC's flagship nightly news program, featuring the day's top stories with in-depth journalism.

programme.title: CBC News NL
programme.desc: Bringing you breaking news, in-depth reports and weather you can count on.

programme.title: Experiencing Canada
programme.desc: Join Nathan in Nova Scotia to uncover the surf, sail the Bluenose II, and savor fresh oysters and lobster.

programme.title: Experiencing Canada
programme.desc: In Quebec, Nathan kayaks the Saguenay Fjord, unwinds at a distillery, and hunts for quartz crystals.

programme.title: Your Weather Today
programme.desc: National and local forecast details for across the country

programme.title: Force of Nature Rewind
programme.desc: Wild and viral weather footage from across Canada and around the world

programme.title: Your Weather Today
programme.desc: National and local forecast details for across the country

programme.title: Force of Nature Rewind
programme.desc: Wild and viral weather footage from across Canada and around the world

programme.title: Storm Hunters
programme.desc: Mark & Jaclyn uncover stories behind the disaster, recovery, and tenacious spirit of people in Moore, Oklahoma

programme.title: Force of Nature Rewind
programme.desc: Wild and viral weather footage from across Canada and around the world

programme.title: Power To The People
programme.desc: Harnessing renewable energy through a geoexchange system to cost effectively heat and cool homes and buildings

programme.title: Out Of This World
programme.desc: Everything you need to know about where the northern lights come from and how they get their colour

programme.title: Force of Nature Rewind
programme.desc: Wild and viral weather footage from across Canada and around the world

programme.title: Force of Nature Rewind
programme.desc: Wild and viral weather footage from across Canada and around the world

programme.title: Survivorman
programme.desc: Forced to land his ultralight aircraft, Les is out to endure the elements in this spectacular locale.

programme.title: History of Life on Earth Journey To The Land
programme.desc: As the first landmasses emerged from prehistoric seas, animals took their first steps on dry land. The explosion of life that followed changed the world forever.

programme.title: Magical Fjords
programme.desc: Fjords are one of the most dramatic landscapes on Earth. But for all their beauty and majesty, the surface spectacle is just the tip of the iceberg.

programme.title: Frontier Vets
programme.desc: Roxanne let her puppy case, ‘Ripple' stay overnight at the dorm. The group – especially her roommate Charlotte - is critical of this. Not only is she using the dog as a social crutch, it's highly unprofessional to become so attached to a patient.

programme.title: Frontier Vets
programme.desc: Dr. Greg forces the students to attend a local Heritage Festival – something none of them are in the mood for on their ‘day off'. But will their attitudes change after witnessing the South African dance and folk customs?

programme.title: Wild Madagascar
programme.desc: Breaking off from mainland Africa 160 million years ago, refugee animals cast adrift on natural rafts arrived on the tropical shores of Madagascar.

programme.title: Extinction! Going, Going, Gone
programme.desc: Extinction, a daunting and confusing word. A force of nature, that sees the end of species, but also heralds the birth of others. Right now the extinction rate could be as much as 10,000 times faster than it should be. 20% of fish, a fifth of all reptiles and a quarter of mammals face extinction. If we are to do anything to stem the tide of extinction we must understand the lifestyles of those in danger. The future for countless animals is uncertain, but one thing is for sure – we have a lot to learn.

programme.title: History of Life on Earth Journey To The Land
programme.desc: As the first landmasses emerged from prehistoric seas, animals took their first steps on dry land. The explosion of life that followed changed the world forever.

programme.title: Magical Fjords
programme.desc: Fjords are one of the most dramatic landscapes on Earth. But for all their beauty and majesty, the surface spectacle is just the tip of the iceberg.

programme.title: The Universe
programme.desc: One of the Universe's most enduring mysteries is Time Travel.

programme.title: The Universe
programme.desc: They sound like the same phenomenon, but Pulsars and Quasars are very different.

programme.title: The Universe
programme.desc: In a galaxy filled with a billion stars, are we alone?

programme.title: The Universe
programme.desc: In this episode, our experts cook up ten ways you could destroy the earth.

programme.title: The Universe
programme.desc: Death stars are in the final stage of life before they explode into supernovae.

programme.title: The Universe
programme.desc: View what are considered the "crown jewels" of the heavens.

programme.title: The Universe
programme.desc: According to laws of physics we can never travel faster than the speed of light.

programme.title: The Universe
programme.desc: One of the Universe's most enduring mysteries is Time Travel.
